step_by_step_thinking:
I concur with the assessment but would add another important point regarding the nature of the surgery itself. The complex anatomical position often requires extensive bone resection which increases the chances of encountering small vessels leading to higher rates of intra-surgical hemorrhaging. Additionally, the close proximity to the mental foramen further complicates control over minor bleeders, contributing significantly to overall post-extraction haemorrhage. Given all factors involved—technical difficulty, close anatomic relationships, and inherent vascular involvement—the risk of excessive bleeding remains paramount among the listed options.

Therefore, **my final answer is still C**.
